questions.question: Where was Moses speaking from in Deuteronomy 1:1?
questions.correct_answer: In the wilderness on this side Jordan
options: In the wilderness on this side Jordan
questions.explanation: Deuteronomy 1:1 introduces Moses speaking to Israel on this side of the Jordan in the wilderness.

questions.question: How many days' journey from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea are mentioned in Deuteronomy 1:2?
questions.correct_answer: Eleven days' journey
questions.explanation: The verse states there are eleven days' journey from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea.

questions.question: In which month and on what day did Moses speak to Israel in the fortieth year?
questions.correct_answer: Eleventh month, first day
questions.explanation: Deuteronomy 1:3 says Moses spoke in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first day of the month.

questions.question: Which two Amorite kings did Moses say he had slain in Deuteronomy 1:4?
questions.correct_answer: Sihon and Og
questions.explanation: Moses refers to Sihon king of the Amorites and Og king of Bashan.

questions.question: On which side of the Jordan did Moses start declaring the law?
questions.correct_answer: On this side Jordan
questions.explanation: Deuteronomy 1:5 says Moses began to declare the law on this side of Jordan.

questions.question: What did the Lord say about their time at Horeb in Deuteronomy 1:6?
questions.correct_answer: Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount
options: Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount
Ye have not yet learned the law
Ye must build an altar here
questions.explanation: God told them they had dwelt long enough at Horeb and should take their journey.

questions.question: Which river is named as the great river in Deuteronomy 1:7?
questions.correct_answer: The river Euphrates
questions.explanation: The verse names the great river as the river Euphrates.

questions.question: Who swore the land to the fathers of Israel in Deuteronomy 1:8?
questions.correct_answer: The LORD
questions.explanation: The verse says the land was sworn unto their fathers by the LORD.

questions.question: Why did Moses say he could not bear them alone in Deuteronomy 1:9?
questions.correct_answer: Because the people were too many and burdensome
options: Because the people were too many and burdensome
Because they wanted to return to Egypt
questions.explanation: Moses stated he could not bear the people alone because of their burden.

questions.question: What analogy does Moses use in Deuteronomy 1:10 for the people of Israel?
questions.correct_answer: As the stars of heaven for multitude
options: As the stars of heaven for multitude
As the sands of the sea
As the grass of the field
As the trees of the forest
questions.explanation: Moses compares Israel's number to the stars of heaven.

questions.question: What did Moses ask the people to take among them in Deuteronomy 1:12?
questions.correct_answer: Wise men and understanding
questions.explanation: Moses asked them to choose wise and understanding men for leaders.

questions.question: What title did Moses give to the chosen leaders in Deuteronomy 1:13?
questions.correct_answer: Heads over you, captains over thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens
options: Heads over you, captains over thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens
questions.explanation: He appointed them as heads and captains over the people.

questions.question: What did Moses tell the judges to do between brothers?
questions.correct_answer: Hear the causes and judge righteously
options: Hear the causes and judge righteously
questions.explanation: Moses instructed judges to hear cases and judge justly.

questions.question: In Deuteronomy 1:17, should judges respect persons?
questions.correct_answer: No, they should not respect persons
options: No, they should not respect persons
questions.explanation: Judges must be impartial and not show favoritism.

questions.question: When the people came to the mountain of the Amorites, what did Moses say in Deuteronomy 1:20?
questions.correct_answer: The Lord has set the land before you; go up and possess it
options: The Lord has set the land before you; go up and possess it
questions.explanation: Moses encouraged them to enter and take possession of the land.

questions.question: What did Moses tell Israel not to do in Deuteronomy 1:21?
questions.correct_answer: Fear not and neither be discouraged
options: Fear not and neither be discouraged
questions.explanation: Moses said they should not fear or be discouraged because the Lord would fight for them.

questions.question: What did Israel say they would do with the spies in Deuteronomy 1:22?
questions.correct_answer: Send men before us to search out the land
options: Send men before us to search out the land
questions.explanation: They asked to send men to scout the land and report back.

questions.question: How many men did Moses choose to spy out the land?
questions.correct_answer: Twelve men
questions.explanation: He chose twelve men, one from each tribe.

questions.question: What did the spies bring back from the land in Deuteronomy 1:25?
questions.correct_answer: Fruit and report that it is a good land
options: Fruit and report that it is a good land
questions.explanation: They brought fruit and said the land was good.

questions.question: What did Israel do instead of going up into the land?
questions.correct_answer: Rebelled against the commandment of the LORD
options: Rebelled against the commandment of the LORD
questions.explanation: They refused to go in and were rebellious.

questions.question: What did the people say when they murmured in their tents?
questions.correct_answer: The Lord has brought us forth to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites
options: The Lord has brought us forth to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites
We will trust the Lord always
We choose to go to Canaan
questions.explanation: They complained that God had taken them out of Egypt only to be destroyed.

questions.question: What did Moses tell Israel not to fear?
questions.correct_answer: Do not be afraid of the people of the land
options: Do not be afraid of the people of the land
Do not be afraid of the sea
Do not be afraid of the wilderness
Do not be afraid of their own hearts
questions.explanation: Moses encouraged them to trust God even though the people were strong.

questions.question: Who did Moses say would fight for Israel?
questions.correct_answer: The LORD your God
questions.explanation: Moses said the LORD would fight for them as He had done in Egypt.

questions.question: What did Israel fail to believe in the wilderness?
questions.correct_answer: They did not believe the LORD their God
options: They did not believe the LORD their God
They did not believe the prophets
They did not believe Moses’ law
They did not believe the Ark
questions.explanation: Moses says they did not trust God despite His guiding presence.

questions.question: Which individual was allowed to see the promised land?
questions.correct_answer: Caleb the son of Jephunneh
questions.explanation: God spared Caleb and allowed him to see the land.

questions.question: Who else was punished along with the people?
questions.correct_answer: Moses was also not permitted to enter
options: Moses was also not permitted to enter
Aaron was not permitted to enter
Joshua was not permitted to enter
Caleb was not permitted to enter
questions.explanation: God said Moses would not go in because of the people’s rebellion.

questions.question: Who would go over Jordan in Moses’ place?
questions.correct_answer: Joshua the son of Nun
questions.explanation: Joshua was chosen to lead Israel into the land.

questions.question: What did the people decide to do after their rebellion?
questions.correct_answer: They said they would go up and fight
options: They said they would go up and fight
They decided to remain in the wilderness
They resolved to return to Egypt
questions.explanation: Despite God’s warning, they armed themselves to fight.

questions.question: What command did the LORD give when the people prepared to fight?
questions.correct_answer: Go not up, neither fight
questions.explanation: God said He would not be among them and they would be defeated.

questions.question: Who defeated Israel at Mount Seir?
questions.correct_answer: The Amorites
questions.explanation: The Amorites came out against Israel and destroyed them in Seir.

questions.question: Which mountain did Israel compass many days in Deuteronomy 2:1?
questions.correct_answer: Mount Seir
questions.explanation: They went through the wilderness by the way of the Red sea and compassed Mount Seir.

questions.question: What were the Israelites told not to do to Esau’s descendants in Deuteronomy 2:5?
questions.correct_answer: Do not meddle with them
questions.explanation: God said He would not give them the land of Esau.

questions.question: What did Israel buy from Esau’s descendants?
questions.correct_answer: Meat and water for money
questions.explanation: They purchased food and water from them.

questions.question: How had the LORD blessed Israel in the wilderness?
questions.correct_answer: They lacked nothing
questions.explanation: The verse says the Lord blessed their works and they lacked nothing.

questions.question: What city did God say not to distress or contend with?
questions.correct_answer: Moab
questions.explanation: God commanded them not to distress Moab or contend with them.

questions.question: What people were described as giants in Deuteronomy 2:11?
questions.correct_answer: The Emims
questions.explanation: The verse says the Emims were accounted giants.

questions.question: Which king did Israel begin to possess when they passed over the river Arnon?
questions.correct_answer: Sihon
questions.explanation: They were told to begin to possess the land of Sihon king of Heshbon.

questions.question: Which king opposed Israel at Edrei in Deuteronomy 3:1?
questions.correct_answer: Og king of Bashan
questions.explanation: Og king of Bashan came out against Israel at Edrei.

questions.question: What had the LORD told Moses about Og in Deuteronomy 3:2?
questions.correct_answer: Fear him not; for I will deliver him into thy hand
options: Fear him not; for I will deliver him into thy hand
questions.explanation: God told Moses not to fear Og because He would deliver him.

questions.question: What did Israel do to the cities of Bashan?
questions.correct_answer: Utterly destroyed them
options: Captured them but spared the people
questions.explanation: They destroyed the cities as they had done to Sihon.

questions.question: How long was Og's bedstead according to Deuteronomy 3:11?
questions.correct_answer: Nine cubits
questions.explanation: Og's bedstead was described as nine cubits long.

questions.question: Which tribes received the land of Gilead?
questions.correct_answer: Reuben and Gad
questions.explanation: Moses gave the land of Gilead to the Reubenites and Gadites.

questions.question: Where was the rest of Gilead given?
questions.correct_answer: To the half tribe of Manasseh
options: To the half tribe of Manasseh
questions.explanation: The rest of Gilead went to the half tribe of Manasseh.

questions.question: What did Moses urge Israel to do with the statutes and judgments?
questions.correct_answer: Hear them and do them
questions.explanation: Moses urged Israel to hear the law and obey it.

questions.question: What must Israel not do with God's word?
questions.correct_answer: Add or diminish from it
questions.explanation: The Lord commanded them not to add to or take away from the law.

questions.question: Why was Israel alive according to Deuteronomy 4:4?
questions.correct_answer: Because they cleaved unto the LORD their God
options: Because they cleaved unto the LORD their God
questions.explanation: Those who remained faithful were alive on that day.

questions.question: What had Moses taught Israel as the LORD commanded?
questions.correct_answer: Statutes and judgments
questions.explanation: Moses taught them the statutes and judgments given by God.

questions.question: Who did Moses tell Israel to teach the law to?
questions.correct_answer: Your sons and your sons' sons
options: Your sons and your sons' sons
questions.explanation: Moses urged continual teaching of the commandments to future generations.

questions.question: Where were the ten commandments written according to Deuteronomy 4:13?
questions.correct_answer: On two tables of stone
options: In the book of the law
questions.explanation: God wrote the commandments on stone tablets.

questions.question: What did Moses warn Israel not to make?
questions.correct_answer: A graven image or likeness
questions.explanation: He warned against making any image of a creature.

questions.question: Why did Moses call Israel to hear the statutes and judgments in Deuteronomy 5:1?
questions.correct_answer: So that they may learn, keep, and do them
options: So that they may learn, keep, and do them
So that they may teach them to strangers
So that they may compare them to other laws
So that they may write them down
questions.explanation: Moses wanted the people to learn and obey the law.

questions.question: How did the LORD speak to Israel at Horeb?
questions.correct_answer: Face to face out of the midst of the fire
options: Face to face out of the midst of the fire
questions.explanation: The verse states God spoke directly to Israel from the fire on the mountain.

questions.question: What is the first commandment listed in Deuteronomy 5:7?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt have none other gods before me
options: Thou shalt have none other gods before me
Thou shalt not make unto thee any graven image
Thou shalt not take the name of the LORD thy God in vain
questions.explanation: The first commandment forbids other gods.

questions.question: What day is to be kept holy according to Deuteronomy 5:12?
questions.correct_answer: The sabbath day
questions.explanation: The sabbath is commanded as a holy day of rest.

questions.question: What honor is commanded in Deuteronomy 5:16?
questions.correct_answer: Honor thy father and thy mother
options: Honor thy father and thy mother
questions.explanation: The fifth commandment instructs honor for parents.

questions.question: Which act is forbidden by Deuteronomy 5:17?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt not kill
questions.explanation: The sixth commandment prohibits killing.

questions.question: What does Deuteronomy 5:18 forbid?
questions.correct_answer: Adultery
questions.explanation: The verse prohibits committing adultery.

questions.question: What is forbidden by Deuteronomy 5:19?
questions.correct_answer: Stealing
questions.explanation: The commandment prohibits theft.

questions.question: What does Deuteronomy 5:20 command?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor
options: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor
Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's house
questions.explanation: It forbids giving false testimony.

questions.question: Which desire is prohibited by Deuteronomy 5:21?
questions.correct_answer: Coveting thy neighbor's wife
questions.explanation: The commandment forbids coveting a neighbor's spouse.

questions.question: What declaration begins in Deuteronomy 6:4?
questions.correct_answer: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD
options: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God is one LORD
Do not make any graven image
questions.explanation: This verse states the Shema, declaring God's oneness.

questions.question: How are Israelites told to love the LORD in Deuteronomy 6:5?
questions.correct_answer: With all their heart, soul, and might
options: With all their heart, soul, and might
questions.explanation: The verse commands wholehearted love for God.

questions.question: Where should the words of God be placed according to Deuteronomy 6:6?
questions.correct_answer: In your heart
questions.explanation: God's words should be held close in the heart.

questions.question: When should parents teach God's commandments to their children?
questions.correct_answer: When sitting, walking, lying down, and rising up
options: When sitting, walking, lying down, and rising up
questions.explanation: They should teach them throughout daily life.

questions.question: What are Israelites instructed to bind as a sign upon their hand?
questions.correct_answer: God's commandments
questions.explanation: The verse says to bind the commandments as a sign on the hand.

questions.question: Where else does Deuteronomy 6:9 say to write God's words?
questions.correct_answer: On the doorposts of your house and on your gates
options: On the doorposts of your house and on your gates
On the altar of the Lord
On the stones of the temple
On the pillars of the city
questions.explanation: This verse commands writing His words at home and gate entry points.

questions.question: What did Moses say about tempting the LORD in Deuteronomy 6:16?
questions.correct_answer: Ye shall not tempt the LORD your God
options: Ye shall not tempt the LORD your God
Ye shall test the LORD with signs
questions.explanation: The people were forbidden to put God to the test.

questions.question: Why did God command these statutes, according to Deuteronomy 6:24?
questions.correct_answer: For our good always
options: For the strangers in the land
questions.explanation: The statutes were given for Israel's continual good.

questions.question: How many nations did Israel have to dispossess in Deuteronomy 7:1?
questions.correct_answer: Seven nations
questions.explanation: The verse lists seven nations before them.

questions.question: What were they commanded not to do with these nations?
questions.correct_answer: Make no covenant with them nor show mercy
options: Make no covenant with them nor show mercy
questions.explanation: They were told to destroy the nations and show no mercy.

questions.question: What kind of marriages were forbidden in Deuteronomy 7:3?
questions.correct_answer: Marriages with the nations of the land
options: Marriages with the nations of the land
questions.explanation: They were forbidden to intermarry with the inhabitants of Canaan.

questions.question: Why had the LORD chosen Israel according to Deuteronomy 7:6?
questions.correct_answer: Because the LORD loved them and kept His oath to their fathers
options: Because the LORD loved them and kept His oath to their fathers
Because they were the strongest nation
Because they were righteous from birth
questions.explanation: God chose them out of love and to keep His covenant.

questions.question: What does the LORD keep for those who love Him and keep His commandments?
questions.correct_answer: His covenant and mercy
questions.explanation: God keeps covenant and mercy with those who love Him.

questions.question: What will happen if Israel hearkens diligently to God's commandments?
questions.correct_answer: The LORD will keep unto them the covenant and mercy
options: The LORD will keep unto them the covenant and mercy
questions.explanation: Obedience ensures God's covenant and mercy.

questions.question: What will the LORD do to the nations that Israel fears?
questions.correct_answer: Send the hornet among them until they are destroyed
options: Send the hornet among them until they are destroyed
questions.explanation: God promised to put fear in the nations and send hornets against them.

questions.question: Why did God humble Israel in the wilderness according to Deuteronomy 8:2?
questions.correct_answer: To know what was in their heart and if they would keep His commandments
options: To know what was in their heart and if they would keep His commandments
questions.explanation: The wilderness was a time of testing and teaching.

questions.question: What did God feed Israel with in the wilderness?
questions.correct_answer: Manna
questions.explanation: God gave them manna to teach dependence on His word.

questions.question: What is God's action toward His people compared to in Deuteronomy 8:5?
questions.correct_answer: As a man chasteneth his son
options: As a man chasteneth his son
As a shepherd cares for his sheep
As a king rules his land
As a father provides for his household
questions.explanation: God disciplines them like a father disciplines his child.

questions.question: What kind of land did Moses describe in Deuteronomy 8:7-8?
questions.correct_answer: A land of brooks of water, fountains, wheat, barley, vines, fig trees, and pomegranates
options: A land of brooks of water, fountains, wheat, barley, vines, fig trees, and pomegranates
A desert land with little water
A land of mountains and snow
A land of cities and merchants
questions.explanation: The land was described as fertile and rich.

questions.question: What warning does Moses give in Deuteronomy 8:11?
questions.correct_answer: Beware that thou forget not the LORD thy God
options: Beware that thou forget not the LORD thy God
Beware of the kings of the land
questions.explanation: They were warned not to forget God when prosperous.

questions.question: Who gives power to get wealth according to Deuteronomy 8:18?
questions.correct_answer: The LORD thy God
questions.explanation: Wealth comes from God's empowering rather than human ability alone.

questions.question: What challenge does Israel face in Deuteronomy 9:1?
questions.correct_answer: To possess nations greater and mightier than themselves
options: To possess nations greater and mightier than themselves
To cross the Jordan in flood season
To feed the nation in the wilderness
questions.explanation: The verse highlights the strength of the nations they must conquer.

questions.question: Why did God drive out the nations according to Deuteronomy 9:4?
questions.correct_answer: Because of their wickedness, not for Israel's righteousness
options: Because of their wickedness, not for Israel's righteousness
Because Israel had a larger army
questions.explanation: God acted on the nations’ wickedness, not Israel's merit.

questions.question: What does Moses remind Israel of in Deuteronomy 9:7?
questions.correct_answer: How they provoked the LORD in the wilderness
options: How they provoked the LORD in the wilderness
questions.explanation: Moses reminds them of their repeated rebellion.

questions.question: What did Moses do with the stone tablets after seeing the golden calf?
questions.correct_answer: Cast them out of his hands and brake them before their eyes
options: Cast them out of his hands and broke them before their eyes
Put them back in the ark
questions.explanation: Moses broke the tablets in anger at the idolatry.

questions.question: What did the Lord do after Moses hewed two tables of stone?
questions.correct_answer: Wrote on them the ten commandments
options: Wrote on them the ten commandments
Commanded Moses to keep them hidden
questions.explanation: God rewrote the ten commandments on new tablets.

questions.question: Who died at Beeroth of the children of Jaakan?
questions.correct_answer: Aaron
questions.explanation: Aaron died there and was buried, with Eleazar succeeding him.

questions.question: Why did the LORD separate the tribe of Levi?
questions.correct_answer: To bear the ark and minister before the LORD
options: To bear the ark and minister before the LORD
questions.explanation: Levi was set apart to serve in the tabernacle.

questions.question: What does the LORD require of Israel in Deuteronomy 10:12?
questions.correct_answer: To fear the LORD, walk in His ways, love Him, serve Him with all their heart and soul
options: To fear the LORD, walk in His ways, love Him, serve Him with all their heart and soul
To build altars in every city
questions.explanation: The verse summarizes God's requirements in a heart-centered obedience.

questions.question: What belongs to the LORD according to Deuteronomy 10:14?
questions.correct_answer: Heaven, the heaven of heavens, and the earth
options: Heaven, the heaven of heavens, and the earth
questions.explanation: God owns all creation, not just Israel.

questions.question: What does God tell Israel to circumcise?
questions.correct_answer: The foreskin of their heart
questions.explanation: God calls for spiritual circumcision of heart.

questions.question: How is God described in Deuteronomy 10:17?
questions.correct_answer: God of gods and Lord of lords
options: God of gods and Lord of lords
questions.explanation: He is supreme over all rulers and gods.

questions.question: What does God do for the fatherless, widow, and stranger?
questions.correct_answer: Executes judgment and loves them
questions.explanation: God cares for the vulnerable and gives them justice.

questions.question: Why should Israel love the stranger?
questions.correct_answer: Because they were strangers in Egypt
options: Because they were strangers in Egypt
questions.explanation: They are reminded of their own history as foreigners.

questions.question: What does Moses tell Israel to love according to Deuteronomy 11:1?
questions.correct_answer: The LORD and keep His charge
options: The LORD and keep His charge
questions.explanation: The verse calls for love of God and obedience.

questions.question: What had the Israelites seen with their eyes according to Deuteronomy 11:7?
questions.correct_answer: All the great acts of the LORD in Egypt and the wilderness
options: All the great acts of the LORD in Egypt and the wilderness
Only the giving of the law
questions.explanation: They had witnessed God's power and wonders firsthand.

questions.question: Why should Israel keep the commandments in Deuteronomy 11:8?
questions.correct_answer: To be strong and possess the land
options: To be strong and possess the land
questions.explanation: Obedience is linked to strength and possession of the land.

questions.question: How is the land of Canaan described compared to Egypt?
questions.correct_answer: It is not like Egypt; it is a land of hills and valleys that drinks rain
options: It is not like Egypt; it is a land of hills and valleys that drinks rain
It is as fertile as Egypt with Nile irrigation
questions.explanation: Canaan is rain-fed and different from Egypt’s irrigation system.

questions.question: What does God promise if they hearken diligently to His commandments?
questions.correct_answer: To give rain in due season and bless their land
options: To give rain in due season and bless their land
questions.explanation: God promises agricultural blessing for obedience.

questions.question: What must Israel observe in the land according to Deuteronomy 12:1?
questions.correct_answer: The statutes and judgments given by God
options: The statutes and judgments given by God
questions.explanation: They must obey God’s laws in the land they inherit.

questions.question: What was Israel commanded to do to the places where the nations served their gods?
questions.correct_answer: Utterly destroy them and burn their groves
options: Utterly destroy them and burn their groves
Convert them into cities of refuge
questions.explanation: The nations’ shrines were to be removed completely to prevent idolatry.

questions.question: Where were Israelites instructed to bring burnt offerings and sacrifices?
questions.correct_answer: To the place the LORD shall choose
options: To the place the LORD shall choose
To any high place they choose
questions.explanation: God required central worship at the chosen sanctuary.

questions.question: What is forbidden when eating the tithe within your gates?
questions.correct_answer: You may not eat it within your gates if you are ceremonially unclean
options: You may not eat it within your gates if you are ceremonially unclean
You may not eat it on the road
You may not eat it without a sacrifice
You may not eat it after sundown
questions.explanation: The tithe must be eaten in purity before the Lord.

questions.question: What may a person do with meat if the chosen place is too far away?
questions.correct_answer: Kill and eat it in his own gates
options: Kill and eat it in his own gates
Offer it as a sin offering
questions.explanation: They were allowed to eat the offering locally if the chosen place was too distant.

questions.question: What reason is given for not eating blood?
questions.correct_answer: Because the blood is the life
options: Because the blood is the life
Because it is unclean to the touch
Because it belongs to the priest
questions.explanation: Blood is sacred as it represents life.

questions.question: If the chosen place is too far, what may a man do with his offerings?
questions.correct_answer: Turn them into money and eat here
options: Turn them into money and eat here
Leave them at the place forever
Give them to a Levite only
questions.explanation: If travel was impractical, offerings could be converted to money for local use.

questions.question: What was Israel warned not to do once God cut off the nations from before them?
questions.correct_answer: Enquire after the gods of those nations
options: Enquire after the gods of those nations
questions.explanation: They were warned not to be influenced by pagan religious practices.

questions.question: Why should Israel not do so unto the LORD?
questions.correct_answer: Because the nations did abominations to their gods
options: Because the nations did abominations to their gods
Because they were stronger than Israel
Because it was forbidden by the king
questions.explanation: Israel was not to imitate the abominable religious practices of the nations.

questions.question: What should they do if a prophet or dreamer gives a sign?
questions.correct_answer: See if the sign follows and the prophet speaks in the LORD’s name
options: See if the sign follows and the prophet speaks in the LORD’s name
questions.explanation: They must test prophets by whether their words come true and match God's will.

questions.question: If a miracle leads people to serve other gods, what does that indicate?
questions.correct_answer: The prophet is false and should not be hearkened to
options: The prophet is false and should not be hearkened to
The miracle is from the LORD
questions.explanation: False prophets may perform signs to deceive, but they are not from God.

questions.question: What should Israel do to the false prophet in Deuteronomy 13:4?
questions.correct_answer: Walk after the LORD and fear Him
options: Walk after the LORD and fear Him
questions.explanation: They are to remain faithful to God regardless of false signs.

questions.question: What happens to a prophet who speaks presumptuously in God's name?
questions.correct_answer: He shall die
questions.explanation: False prophecy in God’s name is punishable by death.

questions.question: Which relatives might entice you to serve another god?
questions.correct_answer: Brother, son, daughter, wife, or friend
options: Brother, son, daughter, wife, or friend
questions.explanation: Close relations may secretly entice a person away from God.

questions.question: What must be done if such an enticement is confirmed?
questions.correct_answer: Stone the enticer to death
questions.explanation: The law requires a severe penalty for leading Israel into idolatry.

questions.question: What physical signs were Israelites forbidden to make for the dead?
questions.correct_answer: Cut themselves or make baldness between their eyes
options: Cut themselves or make baldness between their eyes
questions.explanation: They were to remain distinct from pagan mourning customs.

questions.question: What directive is given in Deuteronomy 14:3 about eating?
questions.correct_answer: Thou shalt not eat any abominable thing
options: Thou shalt not eat any abominable thing
Thou shalt not eat meat on the sabbath
Thou shalt not eat foreign food
Thou shalt not eat unleavened bread
questions.explanation: The law forbids eating ritually unclean animals.

questions.question: What is prohibited about birds in Deuteronomy 14:15?
questions.correct_answer: You shall not eat the owl, night hawk, or other unclean birds
options: You shall not eat the owl, night hawk, or other unclean birds
You shall not eat any birds at all
You shall not eat birds with wings
You shall only eat birds in sacrifice
questions.explanation: Certain birds are listed as unclean for eating.

questions.question: What should you do if an animal dies of itself?
questions.correct_answer: Give it to a stranger or sell it, but do not eat it
options: Give it to a stranger or sell it, but do not eat it
questions.explanation: Animals that die naturally are unclean and cannot be eaten.

questions.question: How often must creditors release debt according to Deuteronomy 15:1?
questions.correct_answer: At the end of every seven years
options: At the end of every seven years
questions.explanation: The law of release applied every seventh year.

questions.question: What must a wealthy person not do to the poor brother in need?
questions.correct_answer: Not harden his heart or shut his hand
options: Not harden his heart or shut his hand
questions.explanation: Generosity is commanded toward the poor.

questions.question: What must an Israelite do to a Hebrew servant after six years?
questions.correct_answer: Let him go free
questions.explanation: Hebrew servants were to be released in the seventh year.

questions.question: If a servant loves his master and does not want to leave, what should be done?
questions.correct_answer: Thrust an aul through his ear and let him serve forever
options: Thrust an aul through his ear and let him serve forever
Sell him back to his family
questions.explanation: A permanent servant was ceremonially marked by an aul through the ear.

questions.question: What must be done with the firstling of herd and flock?
questions.correct_answer: Sanctify it unto the LORD
questions.explanation: The firstborn animals were to be dedicated to God.

questions.question: What must a person do with the blood of an animal?
questions.correct_answer: Pour it upon the ground as water
options: Pour it upon the ground as water
questions.explanation: The blood was not to be eaten but poured out on the ground.

questions.question: What month is the Passover observed?
questions.correct_answer: Abib
questions.explanation: The Passover is celebrated in the month of Abib.

questions.question: What were Israelites forbidden to eat during the Passover?
questions.correct_answer: Leavened bread
questions.explanation: They were commanded to eat unleavened bread for seven days.

questions.question: Where was the Passover to be kept?
questions.correct_answer: In the place which the LORD shall choose
options: In the place which the LORD shall choose
In every gate of the city
questions.explanation: Centralized worship at the chosen sanctuary was required.

questions.question: How many weeks are to be numbered from the reaping of the harvest?
questions.correct_answer: Seven weeks
questions.explanation: The Feast of Weeks is counted by seven weeks from the beginning of harvest.

questions.question: Who was to rejoice before the LORD at the Feast of Weeks?
questions.correct_answer: You, your sons, daughters, servants, Levites, strangers, fatherless, and widows
options: You, your sons, daughters, servants, Levites, strangers, fatherless, and widows
questions.explanation: The entire community, including the marginalized, was to rejoice together.

questions.question: What kind of officials were to be appointed in every city?
questions.correct_answer: Judges and officers that judge with just judgment
options: Judges and officers that judge with just judgment
questions.explanation: Judicial officials were to administer justice fairly.

questions.question: What must not be done in judgment?
questions.correct_answer: Pervert judgment or respect persons
questions.explanation: Judges must avoid bias and corruption.

questions.question: What kind of tree must not be planted near the altar?
questions.correct_answer: A grove of any trees
questions.explanation: Trees were not to be planted near the altar to avoid idolatry.

questions.question: What is forbidden when offering sacrifice to the LORD?
questions.correct_answer: Offering any blemished animal
questions.explanation: Sacrifices must be without blemish.

questions.question: What should be done if a man or woman is found worshiping other gods?
questions.correct_answer: Bring them to the gate and stone them to death
options: Bring them to the gate and stone them to death
questions.explanation: Idolatry is punished severely under the Mosaic law.

questions.question: How many witnesses are required to convict someone of a capital crime?
questions.correct_answer: Two or three witnesses
questions.explanation: The law requires multiple witnesses for capital cases.

questions.question: Where is one to go if a matter is too hard for local judges?
questions.correct_answer: To the place the LORD shall choose
options: To the place the LORD shall choose
To the king of another nation
questions.explanation: Difficult cases were referred to the central sanctuary.

questions.question: Who should decide the difficult cases at the chosen place?
questions.correct_answer: The priests the Levites and the judge in those days
options: The priests the Levites and the judge in those days
questions.explanation: The central judges at the sanctuary rendered decisions.

questions.question: What were the people commanded to do with the judge’s sentence?
questions.correct_answer: Do according to what he tells them
options: Do according to what he tells them
Ignore it if it seems harsh
questions.explanation: Israel was to obey the verdict of the appointed judge.

questions.question: What will happen if Israel disobeys the sentence of the judge?
questions.correct_answer: The evil will remain among them
options: The evil will remain among them
They will receive a second chance
questions.explanation: Ignoring righteous judgment leaves sin in the community.

questions.question: When Israel desires a king, what kind of king should they appoint?
questions.correct_answer: One chosen by the LORD from among their brothers
options: One chosen by the LORD from among their brothers
questions.explanation: The king must be an Israelite chosen by God.

questions.question: What must the king not multiply to himself?
questions.correct_answer: Horses, wives, or silver and gold
options: Horses, wives, or silver and gold
questions.explanation: The king should not amass horses, wives, or wealth.

questions.question: Why should the king not multiply wives to himself?
questions.correct_answer: Lest his heart turn away
options: Lest he lose the respect of people
questions.explanation: Many wives could lead the king away from God.

questions.question: What should the king do after taking the throne?
questions.correct_answer: Write a copy of the law and read it all his life
options: Write a copy of the law and read it all his life
questions.explanation: The king must keep the law before him continually.

questions.question: Why should the king read the law daily?
questions.correct_answer: So he may learn to fear the LORD and keep all His words
options: So he may learn to fear the LORD and keep all His words
So he may impress foreign nations
questions.explanation: Daily reading keeps the king humble and obedient to God.

questions.question: What will be the result if the king follows the law?
questions.correct_answer: His heart will not be lifted above his brethren
options: His heart will not be lifted above his brethren
questions.explanation: The king should remain humble and not exalt himself.

questions.question: Who among Israel had no inheritance in the land?
questions.correct_answer: The priests the Levites
questions.explanation: The Levites’ inheritance was the Lord Himself and their priestly service.

questions.question: Which practices were Israelites forbidden to learn from the nations?
questions.correct_answer: Child sacrifice, divination, enchantment, witchcraft, necromancy
options: Child sacrifice, divination, enchantment, witchcraft, necromancy
questions.explanation: These pagan practices were abominations.

questions.question: What type of prophet did Moses say the LORD would raise up?
questions.correct_answer: A prophet like Moses
questions.explanation: God promised a prophet from among their brethren like Moses.

questions.question: What will God put in the mouth of the coming prophet?
questions.correct_answer: His words
questions.explanation: God promised to place His words in the prophet’s mouth.

questions.question: What is required of those who will not hearken to the LORD’s prophet?
questions.correct_answer: God will require it of them
options: God will require it of them
They will be abandoned to idols
They will be sent into exile
questions.explanation: Disobedience to God’s appointed prophet carries personal responsibility before God.
